message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "architecture" reviewer, severity major:
TITLE: Backend selection fails open on isolation — silent fallback to weakest backend
DETAIL: src/backend.rs `select()` silently degrades: macOS without Tart falls all the way through to plain `Backend::Docker`; Linux without KVM returns `DockerSysbox` with the comment 'assume Sysbox is installed or will be' — an assumption, not a check — and nothing verifies Docker itself, sysbox-runc, or libvirtd exist. `has_kvm` (a bare /dev/kvm stat) is also an insufficient proxy for 'libvirt is a sane default': libvirt requires a daemon, storage pools, networks, and images — a large setup burden on a typical Linux box where Docker is already running — yet the design auto-selects it with no prerequisite validation and no user confirmation. Worst, the selection isn't persisted-and-pinned: since `select()` is derived from live probes at init time, an environment change (tart uninstalled, /dev/kvm missing after a kernel update) would silently change the isolation class of a security-sensitive system. A tool whose stated purpose is 'strongest practical isolation' must fail closed: refuse to run with weaker isolation than configured unless the operator explicitly opts in.
RECOMMENDATION: Persist the chosen backend in config at init; at start, validate full prerequisites for that backend (daemon reachable, image available, sysbox runtime registered) and hard-fail with actionable instructions if missing. Fallback to a weaker isolation class must require an explicit flag/config (`isolation: allow-downgrade: true` or `--allow-container-isolation`), and plain Docker (no Sysbox) should arguably not be auto-selectable at all. Also reconsider libvirt-by-default vs Docker+Sysbox-by-default on Linux given the setup burden, or make `init` interactive about it.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

input.description: Inspect design doc for backend references
List repo root and src dir

content.content: [REDACTED for publication: verbatim content from a private repository (worldarchitect.ai self-hosted runner infrastructure) was returned by this tool call and has been removed. The reviewer's analysis of it is preserved in subsequent assistant messages.]

toolUseResult: [REDACTED for publication: verbatim content from a private repository (worldarchitect.ai self-hosted runner infrastructure) was returned by this tool call and has been removed. The reviewer's analysis of it is preserved in subsequent assistant messages.]

input.reasoning: Verified against the design doc's actual skeleton code. Core claims are factually correct: (1) select() on macOS without Tart does fall through to plain Backend::Docker (lines 195-206); (2) Linux without KVM returns DockerSysbox with the literal comment "assume Sysbox is installed or will be" (line 203); (3) the skeleton's only capability probes are a bare /dev/kvm existence check and `which tart` (platform.rs, lines 179-180) — no verification of Docker daemon, sysbox-runc, or libvirtd; (4) /dev/kvm existence is genuinely an insufficient proxy for libvirt readiness (daemon, storage pools, networks, images all required). The doc's Core Principles state "strongest practical isolation" while the selection logic fails open to the weakest backend, so the fail-closed recommendation (persist+validate prerequisites at start, explicit opt-in flag for isolation downgrade) is sound and severity=major is justified for a security-isolation tool. Two sub-claims are overstated but do not invalidate the finding: (a) the "selection isn't persisted" claim is partially wrong — select() runs only at Init and config::generate() writes the chosen backend into outrunner.yml, which start consumes, so the backend IS persisted; the real gap is only that start never re-validates prerequisites and re-running init silently re-selects; (b) "nothing verifies prerequisites" ignores that the design's Data Flow step 4 explicitly lists "Ensure prerequisites (gha-outrunner binary, Sysbox/Tart, etc.)" and Next Steps item 4 is prerequisite installation logic — the design contemplates it, the skeleton just doesn't implement it.
input.corrected_claim: Backend selection fails open on isolation: select() silently degrades (macOS without Tart → plain Docker; Linux without KVM → DockerSysbox on an unchecked assumption), and the only capability probes are a /dev/kvm stat and `which tart` — insufficient proxies for libvirt/Sysbox/Docker readiness. Correction to two sub-claims: the chosen backend IS persisted (init writes it into outrunner.yml, which start consumes), so post-init environment drift would more likely cause a loud start failure than a silent isolation-class change — the actual gaps are that `start` never validates the pinned backend's prerequisites and re-running `init` silently re-selects; also, the design's Data Flow does list "Ensure prerequisites" as a planned start step (unimplemented in the skeleton), so prerequisite checking is missing from the code, not from the design intent. The recommendation stands: validate full prerequisites for the pinned backend at start, hard-fail with actionable instructions, and require an explicit flag/config to run with a weaker isolation class than configured.
